fields.title: agent-awareness: detect concurrent pi processes (sandbox-coupled)
fields.description:
## Overview

Add an agent-awareness feature to the unified `extensions/tau` extension (not a separate extension package). It should be coupled with `sandbox/` since the behavior is conceptually part of safety/guardrails.

## Problem

When multiple pi agents work concurrently in overlapping directories (especially the same git repo), destructive operations can interfere with each other.

## Solution

Inject awareness into the model context, similar to sandbox's `SANDBOX_STATE:`/`SANDBOX_CHANGE:` notices.

## Location

Implement under:
- `extensions/tau/src/sandbox/agent-awareness/`

and wire from `extensions/tau/src/sandbox/index.ts`.

## Detection Logic

1. Find all pi processes via `pgrep -x pi`
2. For each PID (excluding `process.pid`), read `/proc/<pid>/cwd`
3. Compute our repo root via `discoverWorkspaceRoot(ctx.cwd)`
4. Count other PIDs where:
   - cwd is in same repo root, OR
   - cwd is ancestor/descendant of our cwd
5. If count > 0, inject a notice; if count drops to 0 (was >0), inject a notice.

## Injection Pattern

- System prompt (first agent turn only): safety instructions
- Context hook: prepend `AGENT_CONTEXT: ...` to the last user message content

## Platform Support

- Linux: full support via `/proc`
- macOS/Windows: return 0 / no-op (for now)

## DoD

- No new extension package; lives inside `extensions/tau`
- Works without startup logs
- Notice injects only on change (0↔>0 or count change)
- Manual smoke test passes
fields.close_reason: Implemented sandbox-coupled agent-awareness feature inside extensions/tau with detection + AGENT_CONTEXT injection + tests
